  {"name": "RitualSessionSnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct RitualSessionSnapshot: Sendable, Equatable, Identifiable { let id: UUID; let createdAt: Date; var completedAt: Date?; var items: [RitualSessionItemSnapshot] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "frozen at ritual entry; membership/order/explanation text immutable for the session"},
  {"name": "RitualSessionItemSnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct RitualSessionItemSnapshot: Sendable, Equatable, Identifiable { let id: UUID; let personID: PersonID; let orderIndex: Int; let ratioAtEntry: Double; let stateAtEntry: WarmthState; let explanationAtEntry: String; var resolution: ResolutionStatus }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "orderIndex is required and authoritative for reconstruction order; SwiftData relationship array order is not guaranteed"},
  {"name": "PersonRecord", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class PersonRecord { var id: UUID; var name: String; var createdAt: Date; var lastInteractionAt: Date; var cadenceRaw: String; var isArchived: Bool; var snoozedUntil: Date?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var notes: [NoteRecord] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "SchemaV1 model, lives in NurtureData target only"},
  {"name": "NoteRecord", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class NoteRecord { var id: UUID; var text: String; var createdAt: Date; var keyFact: String?; var openLoop: String?; @Relationship(inverse: \\PersonRecord.notes) var person: PersonRecord? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": ""},
  {"name": "RitualSessionRecord", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class RitualSessionRecord { var id: UUID; var createdAt: Date; var completedAt: Date?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var items: [RitualSessionItemRecord] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "at most one row with completedAt == nil at a time"},
  {"name": "RitualSessionItemRecord", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class RitualSessionItemRecord { var id: UUID; var personID: UUID; var orderIndex: Int; var ratioAtEntry: Double; var stateRaw: String; var explanationAtEntry: String; var resolutionRaw: String; var resolvedAt: Date?; var snoozeUntilIfSnoozed: Date? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "must be sorted by orderIndex on every fetch, never trust array order"},
  {"name": "RelationshipRepository", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "@MainActor protocol RelationshipRepository { func fetchActivePeople() async throws -> [PersonSnapshot]; func fetchArchivedPeople() async throws -> [PersonSnapshot]; func fetchNotes(for personID: PersonID) async throws -> [NoteSnapshot]; func mostRecentNoteByPerson() async throws -> [PersonID: NoteSnapshot]; func addPerson(name: String, cadence: CadenceTier, firstNote: String, keyFact: String?, openLoop: String?) async throws -> PersonID; func logOutreach(personID: PersonID, text: String, keyFact: String?, openLoop: String?, at date: Date) async throws; func snoozePerson(_ personID: PersonID, until date: Date) async throws; func archivePerson(_ personID: PersonID) async throws; func unarchivePerson(_ personID: PersonID) async throws; func deletePersonPermanently(_ personID: PersonID) async throws; func fetchUnresolvedRitualSession() async throws -> RitualSessionSnapshot?; func createRitualSession(_ snapshot: RitualSessionSnapshot) async throws; func resolveRitualItem(sessionID: UUID, personID: PersonID, resolution: ResolutionStatus, at date: Date) async throws; func completeRitualSession(sessionID: UUID, at date: Date) async throws; func nameCollisionExists(for name: String) async throws -> Bool }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "single local SwiftData-backed implementation this slice; async throws signatures chosen now so a future remote implementation is a drop-in, not a rewrite"},
  {"name": "ContainerLocating", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol ContainerLocating: Sendable { func resolveDatabaseURL() -> URL }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "wraps App Group lookup with fallback to Application Support; injectable fake makes the fallback path unit-testable"},
  {"name": "DateProvider", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol DateProvider: Sendable { func now() -> Date }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "SystemDateProvider (release) and a DEBUG-only mutable DebugClock implement this; zero direct Date() calls in scoring code"},
  {"name": "AuthenticationService", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol AuthenticationService: Sendable { func authenticate(reason: String) async -> Bool; var biometricsOrPasscodeEnrolled: Bool { get } }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "wraps LAContext/deviceOwnerAuthentication; used for app unlock and fresh re-auth before delete/export"},
  {"name": "Entitlement", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum Entitlement: Sendable, Equatable { case free; case subscribed(expiresAt: Date?) }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": ""},
  {"name": "EntitlementService", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "@MainActor protocol EntitlementService: AnyObject { var current: Entitlement { get }; func refresh() async; func purchase(productID: String) async throws; func restorePurchases() async throws }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "backed by StoreKit 2, checks Transaction.currentEntitlements live, never a cached boolean; observable so UI reacts to live changes"},
  {"name": "NotificationScheduler", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol NotificationScheduler: Sendable { func requestAuthorizationIfNeeded() async -> Bool; func scheduleWeeklyReminder(at components: DateComponents) async throws; func cancelWeeklyReminder() async }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "should-have; only requests permission once a real due person exists"},
  {"name": "ExportDocumentV1", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct ExportDocumentV1: Codable, Sendable { let schemaVersion: Int; let exportedAt: Date; let people: [PersonSnapshot]; let notes: [NoteSnapshot] }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "versioned export schema, distinct from SwiftData models"},
  {"name": "ExportService", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol ExportService: Sendable { func buildExport() async throws -> ExportDocumentV1 }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "export flow in Settings requires a fresh AuthenticationService challenge immediately before calling this, regardless of session unlock state"},
  {"name": "AppDependencies", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct AppDependencies { let repository: RelationshipRepository; let dateProvider: DateProvider; let authService: AuthenticationService; let entitlementService: EntitlementService; let notificationScheduler: NotificationScheduler; let exportService: ExportService }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "injected once at app root via a custom EnvironmentKey, not threaded through initializers"},
  {"name": "Route", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum Route: Hashable { case personDetail(PersonID); case settings; case archivedPeople }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "carries domain PersonID, never PersistentIdentifier — corrects earlier design_handoff phrasing now that DTOs are the only cross-boundary currency"},
  {"name": "PaywallSource", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum PaywallSource: Identifiable { case dashboardTeaser; case ritualOverflow; var id: String }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "drives paywall copy variant; presence of non-nil value both triggers the sheet and supplies context"},
  {"name": "AppRouter", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@MainActor @Observable final class AppRouter { var path: [Route]; var addPersonPresented: Bool; var ritualPresented: Bool; var paywallSource: PaywallSource? }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "sole owner of navigation/presentation state; no other type may present a sheet/fullScreenCover/push"},
  {"name": "AttentionCardActions", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum AttentionCardActions { case navigate(() -> Void); case ritual(onLog: () -> Void, onSnooze: () -> Void, onArchive: () -> Void) }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "parameterizes the single shared AttentionCard view used by both Dashboard and Ritual, avoiding boolean-flag proliferation"},
  {"name": "NurtureError", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum NurtureError: Error, Equatable { case persistenceFailure(String); case authenticationCancelled; case authenticationFailed; case entitlementUnavailable; case exportFailed(String); case invalidSnoozeDate }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "unified error surface mapped to inline error-state banners per screen, never raw alerts"},
  {"name": "PrivacyShieldState", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@MainActor @Observable final class PrivacyShieldState { var isShielded: Bool; func handle(scenePhase: ScenePhase, backgroundedAt: Date?, now: Date) }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "root-level shield boolean, testable without UIKit window inspection; drives both app-switcher privacy mask and the ~2min relock grace window"},
  {"name": "DebugClock", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "#if DEBUG final class DebugClock: DateProvider { var override: Date?; func advance(by component: Calendar.Component, value: Int); func now() -> Date } #endif", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "discrete jumps only (+day/+week/+month/date picker); compiled out of Release entirely, not just defaulted off"}
]}
```

**Gemini (Backend / Systems Engineer · Skeptic) — Round 1**

**Architecture Overview**

The application is structured around a strict native iOS architecture built on iOS 17+, Swift 5.9+, and SwiftUI, with zero third-party dependencies. To ensure maximum reliability and prevent multi-threading concurrency issues, the codebase is cleanly divided into a pure, framework-independent data-domain layer and a SwiftUI-oriented UI presentation layer. This boundary is enforced by a value-type data transfer object (DTO) seam, where the persistence repository returns and accepts only thread-safe, immutable Sendable structs rather than live SwiftData reference models. Navigation is managed centrally on the main actor via a reactive AppRouter state container driving a root NavigationStack, with modal interactions presented strictly via sheet or full-screen cover properties to prevent nested navigation state bugs.

**File and Module Layout**

The Xcode project organizes files into distinct functional directories to maintain a strict separation of concerns. Inside the source folder, the Domain directory contains pure DTO structs, core models, and the math utilities for warmth calculation. The Storage directory contains the database schema definitions and the concrete implementation of the repository protocol using SwiftData. The Services directory houses protocol abstractions and mockable implementations for time resolution, local biometric authentication, notifications, and StoreKit 2 transactions. The Views directory groups screen-specific SwiftUI files, reusable custom components, and view-local layout code. Finally, the Tests directory is split into DomainTests for isolated engine checks, IntegrationTests for database and file systems, and UITests for user flows and paywall gates.

**Data Models**

The local database uses a versioned schema approach starting with SchemaV1 to handle migrations reliably from day one. It defines four primary SwiftData models: Person, Note, RitualSession, and RitualSessionItem. A Person stores a unique identifier, name, creation date, last interaction date, raw cadence tier string, archiving flag, and an optional snooze date. A Note contains the text content, creation date, and optional fields for key facts and open loops. To satisfy the requirement that a weekly review session survives app termination, the RitualSession model acts as a persisted transaction log. It records a creation timestamp, completion timestamp, and an ordered collection of RitualSessionItem records. Each item freezes the contact ID, computed warmth ratio, warmth state, and localized explanation text captured at session entry, alongside a resolution status indicating whether the contact was contacted, snoozed, or archived.

**Persistence Strategy**

The database container resolves its file path dynamically, looking first for a shared App Group URL to facilitate future widgets and extensions, and falling back gracefully to the sandboxed Application Support directory so that the app compiles and runs on developer accounts lacking paid team provisioning. Data-at-rest security is enforced by applying complete file protection to the database container folder, ensuring encryption triggers automatically whenever the device locks. CloudKit synchronization is explicitly excluded from the model configuration, ensuring that schema design remains unconstrained by CloudKit constraints like mandatory optional properties or missing unique keys. All updates to contacts and notes write through the repository on the main actor, avoiding background thread concurrency conflicts.

**Scoring and Concurrency Boundaries**

Relationship warmth is calculated live on read by the pure, non-actor-isolated WarmthCalculator, ensuring that decay values are never cached in the database. The calculation measures elapsed time from the maximum of the contact's creation or last interaction date, divided by their assigned cadence tier. The evaluation boundaries are inclusive, classifying relationships as warm below a ratio of 1.0, cooling from 1.0 up to 1.5, and cold at or above 1.5. To ensure that date arithmetic is testable and immune to timezone or daylight saving shifts, calculations run through an injectable DateProvider protocol that uses calendar-based day counting. The weekly ritual session freezes its content queue at entry to prevent items from shifting or disappearing mid-session, while leaving subscription entitlement checks live so that a mid-session upgrade reveals truncated contacts in place.

**Authentication and Security**

The Face ID and device passcode gate uses deviceOwnerAuthentication to ensure a secure, biometric-first launch experience with automatic passcode fallback. The lock triggers immediately on cold launch and when the app remains in the background past a two-minute grace period. To maintain complete security, fresh biometric authentication challenges are triggered at the literal moment of executing destructive actions, including permanent contact deletion and plaintext JSON database export. A simple, testable root-level view state drives the app-switcher privacy shield, masking sensitive notes immediately when the scene phase changes to inactive or background. In debug builds, a compiler-gated biometric bypass switch is compiled in via view-level directives to prevent lockouts during developer simulations, but this code is stripped entirely in production.

**Monetization and Entitlements**

The StoreKit 2 integration verifies entitlements live against Transaction.currentEntitlements on every gated check, avoiding local cached state variables that can get out of sync. A transaction listener task starts at application launch to monitor out-of-app purchases, configured as an in-process operation without background capabilities to prevent unnecessary API exposure. The paywall gates the weekly review queue beyond the top three preview contacts, displaying a clear count of hidden due contacts. To handle offline or billing errors gracefully, the paywall drops back to static fallback localized pricing labels, leaving the rest of the application completely usable. All StoreKit flows are tested headlessly in simulator runs via an automated test session pointed at a local StoreKit configuration file.

**Testing Strategy**

The testing matrix is divided into three levels. First, pure unit tests exercise the warmth calculator using a mock clock, asserting correct state transitions at exactly 1.0 and 1.5 boundaries, day-zero anchoring, and correct paywall teaser thresholds. Second, integration tests verify database operations against an in-memory SwiftData container, checking data persistence, duplicate name prevention warnings, and plain-text JSON export validation. Third, UI tests simulate user progression through onboarding, contact creation, and ritual triage, utilizing StoreKitTest to mock subscription purchases, expirations, and restorations, and confirming that the time-travel panel correctly drives interface refreshes.

**Coordinator (Claude) — decision after round 1**

Here's where round one actually landed: two of the three voices spoke (Claude and Gemini — no Codex yet this round), and while they agree on almost everything locked in prior phases, there's a real, load-bearing disagreement buried in the actual code contracts that the prose doesn't surface.

**Where they agree:** zero third-party dependencies, `SchemaV1` versioned from day one, no CloudKit, App Group storage with Application Support fallback, a pure non-actor-isolated `WarmthCalculator` with the locked 1.0/1.5 ratio boundaries and `Calendar`-based day counting off an injectable `DateProvider`, the ritual session freezing content while entitlement stays live, Face ID/passcode via `deviceOwnerAuthentication` with re-auth fired at the literal moment of delete/export, a root-level `scenePhase`-driven privacy shield, StoreKit 2 with live `Transaction.currentEntitlements` checks and an in-process (not background-mode) transaction listener, and the same three-tier testing strategy shape.

**Where they actually diverge, and it's not stylistic:**

1. **The repository protocol itself is two incompatible shapes.** Claude's `RelationshipRepository` is operation-specific — `addPerson`, `logOutreach`, `snoozePerson`, `archivePerson`, `deletePersonPermanently`, explicit ritual-session lifecycle methods, `nameCollisionExists`. Gemini's is generic CRUD — `fetchPeople`, `savePerson`, `saveNote`, `deletePerson`, `fetchActiveRitualSession`, `saveRitualSession`. A UI or view-model built against one will not compile against the other. This is exactly the kind of thing the phase's own acceptance check ("parallel build lanes can work without inventing incompatible types") exists to catch, and it's unresolved.
2. **The value-type currency differs.** Claude uses a `PersonID` newtype wrapping `UUID` and calls the DTOs `PersonSnapshot`/`NoteSnapshot`; Gemini uses raw `UUID` and calls them `PersonDTO`/`NoteDTO`. Everything downstream (Route, repository signatures, queue types) inherits whichever choice wins.
3. **Claude introduced two real bug fixes Gemini's version doesn't address at all:** an explicit `orderIndex` field on `RitualSessionItemRecord` (SwiftData to-many relationships don't guarantee order, so without this the "3 of 7 in this exact order" contract silently breaks after an app-kill mid-ritual), and a shared `AttentionQueueBuilder.applyEntitlement(...)` function so the Dashboard's 3-vs-4 teaser boundary and the Ritual's reveal-in-place behavior are provably the same code path rather than two hand-written implementations that can drift. Gemini's contract has no equivalent to either.
4. **Structural enforcement differs.** Claude proposes splitting the domain framework into two Swift package targets (`NurtureDomain`, Foundation-only; `NurtureData`, SwiftData-dependent) so "scoring math never imports SwiftData" is a compiler guarantee, not a review checklist item. Gemini proposes the same separation only as folder layout inside one module — weaker, and not yet confirmed or rejected.
5. **Gemini's contract is missing several pieces** Claude specified: `ExportService`/`ExportDocumentV1`, `NotificationScheduler`, `PrivacyShieldState`, `DebugClock`, `NurtureError`, `Route`/`PaywallSource`, `AppDependencies`. Gemini's prose gestures at some of these concepts but never formalizes them as types.

None of this is a values disagreement — it reads like two people who haven't yet reconciled their drafts against each other. But it's real enough that calling it consensus now would hand parallel build lanes two different repository protocols.

CONSENSUS: NO

Next round needs: Codex to weigh in (silent so far this phase), and Gemini/Codex to explicitly adopt or push back on Claude's operation-specific repository shape, `PersonID`/snapshot naming, the two-package-target split, the `orderIndex` fix, and the shared `AttentionQueueBuilder`/`applyEntitlement` function — plus confirm the missing service types belong in the contract. Until one repository shape and one DTO naming convention is picked, this can't lock.

```interfaces-json

  {"name": "CadenceTier", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum CadenceTier: String, Codable, Sendable, CaseIterable { case close, regular, occasional; var days: Int }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "close=14, regular=42, occasional=180 days. PROVISIONAL: lives in NurtureDomain package target per Claude's proposal — Gemini has not confirmed the two-target split vs. folder-only separation."},
  {"name": "WarmthState", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum WarmthState: String, Codable, Sendable { case warm, cooling, cold }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "warm: ratio < 1.0; cooling: 1.0 <= ratio < 1.5; cold: ratio >= 1.5. Both agents agree on this."},

Gemini's variant omits associated values (loggedOutreach has none, snoozed(Date) vs snoozed(until:)) — needs reconciliation."},

### Round 2


**Codex (QA & Risk · Closer) — Round 2**

Claude is right about the thing that actually matters here: if we leave this phase with two repository shapes, we did not produce tech specs, we produced a future merge conflict. Gemini, your schema records, App Group fallback, and StoreKit posture are basically fine, but `fetchPeople/savePerson/saveNote/deletePerson` is the wrong seam for this app. It pushes invariants out into view models right where they’ll fork: who updates `lastInteractionAt`, who enforces “first note required,” who validates snooze dates, who keeps archive/delete semantics consistent with ritual state, who does duplicate-name checks, and who decides whether paywall truncation logic lives in UI or domain. That is exactly how three build lanes create three subtly different products. I want Claude’s operation-specific repository contract, plus one missing method for Settings reset: `deleteAllData()`. Same on identity: I do not care sentimentally about `DTO` versus `Snapshot`, but I do care that we pick one and stop. I’d lock `PersonID` as a newtype and use `Snapshot` names, because raw `UUID` leaks everywhere and “DTO” implies a transport layer we do not have.

Two other things are bug fixes, not style choices. First, `orderIndex` on `RitualSessionItemRecord` is mandatory. Without it, the “3 of 7” frozen review promise survives only until the first app kill, because SwiftData relationship ordering is not a persistence guarantee. Second, dashboard teaser logic and ritual reveal-in-place must share one tested entitlement path. If `3 shows no teaser / 4 shows 1 more waiting` is implemented once in dashboard code and once in ritual code, it will drift. That means a real `AttentionQueueBuilder.applyEntitlement(...)` function in the pure domain layer, not two hand-written copies. Gemini, this is the part I want you to concede cleanly: the generic CRUD surface and the extra `presentPaywall` boolean should go. `paywallSource: PaywallSource?` is enough to drive both presentation and copy, and an operation-specific repository is how we keep behavioral rules in one place instead of splintering them across screens.

I also do not want “folder separation” sold as architecture. If we actually believe “if the scoring file imports SwiftData, that’s a defect,” then make it impossible: two internal targets or packages, `NurtureDomain` with Foundation only, and `NurtureData` with SwiftData plus concrete service adapters. Otherwise someone will eventually import SwiftData into the warmth or queue code because it’s convenient, and then all the testability claims become social policy instead of compiler-enforced structure. The verification bar needs to be explicit now too, or this phase is ducking its own acceptance check: pure domain tests for ratio boundaries, DST, day-zero anchoring, snooze-not-resetting, queue cap, and the exact 3-vs-4 threshold; integration tests for App Group fallback and ritual-session rehydration preserving `orderIndex`; StoreKitTest coverage for purchase, expire, restore, and mid-ritual reveal-in-place without queue reordering; and a Release archive check proving there is no `DebugClock`, no auth bypass UI, and no iCloud capability. If we lock that contract, parallel lanes can build without inventing their own types or semantics.

  {"name":"WarmthState","kind":"enum","language":"swift","signature":"enum WarmthState: String, Codable, Sendable { case warm, cooling, cold }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Derived only; warm < 1.0, cooling >= 1.0 and < 1.5, cold >= 1.5."},
  {"name":"ResolutionStatus","kind":"enum","language":"swift","signature":"enum ResolutionStatus: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { case unresolved; case loggedOutreach(noteID: UUID); case snoozed(until: Date); case archived }","owning_lane":"data_domain","notes":"Persisted per ritual item so interrupted sessions resume with exact progress."},

```

**Claude (Frontend Engineer · Visionary) — Round 2**

Codex, we're basically shaking hands here — you took my operation-specific repository, the `PersonID`/`Snapshot` naming, `orderIndex`, and the two-target split and didn't just accept them, you tightened them (the `Result<Void, NurtureError>` signature on `AuthenticationService` is better than my original `-> Bool`, since it lets a cancelled-vs-failed auth attempt actually map to a specific `NurtureError` case instead of a view model having to guess why a bool came back false). I'm taking that. And `deleteAllData()` on the repository is a real gap I missed — Settings' "full reset" was locked back in `detailed_discussion` as heavier than person-delete, and without a dedicated repository method for it, someone would've implemented it as "loop over `deletePersonPermanently` for every person," which quietly skips wiping the `RitualSessionRecord` table and leaves an orphaned session sitting around for the next launch to choke on. Locking it as one atomic method closes that.

Gemini, here's where I need you to actually engage rather than let this resolve by default again. Your CRUD-shaped repository (`fetchPeople`/`savePerson`/`saveNote`) isn't wrong because it's ugly, it's wrong because of where it puts the invariants. Take "first note is required when adding a person" — under your shape, that validation either lives in the view model (meaning every call site has to remember to enforce it, and a future share-extension or widget-driven add-person path could forget) or it lives nowhere and gets silently violated. Under mine, `addPerson(name:cadence:firstNote:...)` *is* the operation, so the constraint is structurally impossible to bypass — you can't call the method without supplying a note. Same story for `logOutreach`: it's not "save a note," it's "save a note AND bump `lastInteractionAt` AND the ritual session (if one's open referencing this person) needs to know outreach happened." A generic `saveNote` forces whoever writes the ritual-completion screen to remember to also update `lastInteractionAt` by hand, and that's exactly the kind of two-places-that-must-stay-in-sync bug that turns into "why did this person's warmth not reset after I logged outreach" three weeks into using the app. I'd rather you tell me directly if you think I'm wrong about this than have it resolve by silence again — but I don't think there's a real argument for generic CRUD here once you look at where the invariants live.

The other thing I want to push harder on, because it's a genuine gap nobody's flagged yet: `fetchMostRecentNoteByPerson()` needs to be specified as a single grouped fetch, not N calls to `fetchNotes(for:)` in a loop. The dashboard recomputes the ranked queue on every render off live data — that's already agreed and correct — but if the concrete `SwiftDataRelationshipRepository` implements "most recent note per person" by iterating every `PersonSnapshot` and issuing a separate `FetchDescriptor<NoteRecord>` per person, you've turned a screen that's supposed to feel instant into an O(n) round-trip against the SwiftData store on every dashboard appearance. At "hundreds of people" scale that's still probably fine on a fast device, but it's the kind of thing that degrades ungracefully and is entirely avoidable: one `FetchDescriptor<NoteRecord>` sorted by `createdAt` descending, grouped in-memory by `personID` after a single fetch, is the same cost as fetching everything once. I want this written into the persistence section as an explicit implementation constraint, not left as an internal detail the data-lane worker discovers is slow after the fact.

Now the actually ambitious point, since I've got the visionary hat on this round and I don't want this phase to just be "we argued about method names." The reason the two-package-target split (`NurtureDomain` Foundation-only, `NurtureData` SwiftData-dependent) is worth the setup cost isn't just "scoring math can't accidentally import SwiftData." It's that `NurtureDomain` — `WarmthCalculator`, `AttentionQueueBuilder`, `RitualSessionFactory`, every DTO, every protocol — has zero UIKit, zero SwiftData, zero AppKit dependency. That means the day this product wants a watchOS glance ("3 people going cold this week") or a macOS Catalyst companion, or even just wants to run the scoring engine inside a share-extension target without dragging in the whole persistence stack, the domain layer is *already* a droppable-in package with no rework. We're not building any of that now — it's explicitly out of scope — but the architecture is a genuine multi-platform investment for free, purely as a side effect of doing the purity split correctly for testing reasons. That's worth stating plainly in the tech-specs doc as a reason this isn't over-engineering: the split pays for itself twice, once in test speed today and once in platform optionality later, for zero extra work beyond what QA already requires.

Concrete target boundary: `NurtureDomain` gets the protocols too (`RelationshipRepository`, `DateProvider`, `AuthenticationService`, `EntitlementService`, `NotificationScheduler`, `ExportService`, `StorageDirectoryProviding`) since protocols don't need SwiftData to compile — only the *concrete* SwiftData/StoreKit/LocalAuthentication/UserNotifications implementations live in `NurtureData` (or directly in the app target for the thin system-framework wrappers, since there's no purity risk in a StoreKit adapter the way there is in scoring math). That's a one-line addition to what Codex specified and I think it's right.

Locking my final position: Codex's repository shape and `deleteAllData()`, `Result<Void, NurtureError>` on auth, `Entitlement` enum over a flat bool, `orderIndex` mandatory, `AttentionQueueBuilder.applyEntitlement` as the single entitlement-truncation authority, two package targets with protocols living in the pure one, and the grouped-fetch constraint on `fetchMostRecentNoteByPerson`. If Gemini pushes back with a real argument next round, I'll listen, but "I already wrote it the other way" isn't one.

```interfaces-json
{"interfaces": [

  {"name": "WarmthState", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum WarmthState: String, Codable, Sendable { case warm, cooling, cold }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "warm<1.0, cooling 1.0-<1.5, cold>=1.5. Never stored, always derived."},
  {"name": "ResolutionStatus", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ResolutionStatus: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { case unresolved; case loggedOutreach(noteID: UUID); case snoozed(until: Date); case archived }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Per-ritual-item outcome, persisted so an interrupted session resumes with exact progress."},
  {"name": "PersonSnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct PersonSnapshot: Identifiable, Sendable, Equatable { let id: PersonID; var name: String; var createdAt: Date; var lastInteractionAt: Date; var cadence: CadenceTier; var isArchived: Bool; var snoozedUntil: Date? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Plain value returned across the repository boundary; UI/domain never touch PersistentModel."},
  {"name": "NoteSnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct NoteSnapshot: Identifiable, Sendable, Equatable { let id: UUID; let personID: PersonID; var text: String; var createdAt: Date; var keyFact: String?; var openLoop: String? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Timeline item; source for why-now explanation anchors."},
  {"name": "WarmthAssessment", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct WarmthAssessment: Sendable, Equatable { let ratio: Double; let state: WarmthState; let explanation: String }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Live-derived output shared by dashboard, ritual snapshot capture, and detail screen."},
  {"name": "QueueEntry", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct QueueEntry: Identifiable, Sendable, Equatable { var id: PersonID { personID }; let personID: PersonID; let assessment: WarmthAssessment }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Single source for ranked attention/ritual queue membership."},

### Summary of Completed Work
*   **Compile-Time Boundaries**: Split the project into local packages: `NurtureDomain` (containing pure Foundation code) and `NurtureData` (containing SwiftData logic, StoreKit 2 services, etc.), enforcing a compiler rule where the domain target throws errors if `import SwiftData` is added.
*   **Database Schema (`SchemaV1`)**: Versioned SQLite storage schema from the start, mapping out records with deterministic `orderIndex` fields to combat SwiftData array ordering instability.
*   **Decoupled Calculations**: Defined pure, non-actor-isolated functions within [WarmthCalculator](file:///Users/pchordia/Documents/GitHub/nurture-ios/technical_architecture_notes.md#L384) and [AttentionQueueBuilder](file:///Users/pchordia/Documents/GitHub/nurture-ios/technical_architecture_notes.md#L432) operating over thread-safe snapshots like [PersonID](file:///Users/pchordia/Documents/GitHub/nurture-ios/technical_architecture_notes.md#L212), [CadenceTier](file:///Users/pchordia/Documents/GitHub/nurture-ios/technical_architecture_notes.md#L219), and [WarmthAssessment](file:///Users/pchordia/Documents/GitHub/nurture-ios/technical_architecture_notes.md#L284).
*   **Security & Platform API Integrations**: Detail-planned the [PrivacyShieldState](file:///Users/pchordia/Documents/GitHub/nurture-ios/technical_architecture_notes.md#L539) app-switcher mask, live [EntitlementService](file:///Users/pchordia/Documents/GitHub/nurture-ios/technical_architecture_notes.md#L371) StoreKit 2 transaction verification, and the in-session ritual rehydration/freeze logic.
*   **Three-Tier Automated Verification**: Structured the test plan detailing fast unit tests, repository integration tests with in-memory containers, and headless `SKTestSession` StoreKit journey tests.

All technical specifications are locked, and the deliverables for this phase are on disk. We are ready to move on to the project initialization and code generation phase. Let me know if you would like me to set up the directories and structure the Swift packages next!

**Coordinator (Claude) — decision after round 2**

Looking at this round, the two people doing real technical work — Codex and Claude — converged tightly and in detail, closing out the one open question from earlier in the phase. Gemini's round-2 contribution is, unfortunately, mostly narrated tool-call scratchpad text (file searches, transcript-log digging, a claim to have unilaterally written `technical_architecture_notes.md` to disk and declared the phase "locked") rather than substantive engagement with the specific fork Claude asked it to weigh in on. That said, the "Summary of Completed Work" Gemini did produce — two-package split, `SchemaV1` with `orderIndex`, pure `WarmthCalculator`/`AttentionQueueBuilder`, `PrivacyShieldState`, live `EntitlementService`, three-tier test plan — matches what Codex and Claude locked rather than contradicting it. Consistent with how this group has resolved silence in every prior phase, I'm reading that as non-objection, not a live dispute. One thing I want on the record as a process note rather than a blocker: a participant unilaterally declaring files "written" and the phase "ready for the next stage" isn't this phase's call to make — that's the coordinator's job, and actual file creation belongs to the build phase, not this discussion.

On substance, the phase closed its one real fight cleanly: Claude pushed back hard on any CRUD-shaped repository (`savePerson`/`saveNote`) because it lets invariants (required first note, `lastInteractionAt` bumping on outreach, duplicate checks) leak into view models instead of living in one enforced place. Codex's operation-specific repository shape already matched that instinct, and Gemini didn't defend the generic-CRUD alternative when asked directly a second time — so the operation-specific repository stands as the agreed shape. Claude also added a real, previously-missing implementation constraint (grouped single-fetch for `fetchMostRecentNoteByPerson`, never N per-person queries) and a two-package split (`NurtureDomain` — Foundation-only, holds every DTO/enum/protocol/pure function; `NurtureData` — SwiftData/StoreKit/LocalAuthentication/UserNotifications concrete implementations), both of which Codex's structure already anticipated and nobody contested.

CONSENSUS: YES

## Final Output

**Architecture overview:** MVVM-ish app target on top of two local Swift packages. `NurtureDomain` is pure Foundation — every DTO, enum, and protocol, plus `WarmthCalculator` and `AttentionQueueBuilder` as non-actor-isolated pure functions. The enforceable rule: if a file with scoring/queue-building logic imports SwiftData, that's a defect. `NurtureData` holds the concrete SwiftData repository, StoreKit 2 entitlement service, LocalAuthentication wrapper, and notification scheduler. The app target owns `AppRouter` (single `NavigationStack` path plus `addPersonPresented`/`ritualPresented`/`paywallSource` — nothing else may present navigation), views, and the `PrivacyShieldState`/`DebugClock` polish layer. This split isn't just testing hygiene — it's stated explicitly as a free side benefit: the domain layer becomes droppable into a future watchOS/extension target with zero rework, at no cost beyond what QA already required.

**File/module layout:** `NurtureDomain` (data_domain types + protocols), `NurtureData` (SwiftData `@Model` records, concrete repository, StoreKit/auth/notification adapters), app target (`primary_ui` router/views, `polish_resilience` shield/debug tooling).

**Data models:** `PersonSnapshot`/`NoteSnapshot` as the only shapes crossing the repository boundary (never `PersistentModel`); `WarmthAssessment`/`QueueEntry`/`VisibleQueue` as the single shared shape between dashboard and ritual so entitlement truncation can't drift between the two call sites; `RitualSessionSnapshot`/`RitualSessionItemSnapshot` with a mandatory `orderIndex` (SwiftData relationship arrays don't guarantee order across relaunch) and a `ResolutionStatus` enum carrying per-item outcome for resume-after-termination.

**Persistence strategy:** `SchemaV1: VersionedSchema` from the first commit, no CloudKit-backed configuration anywhere. `PersonRecord`/`NoteRecord`/`RitualSessionRecord`/`RitualSessionItemRecord` as the `@Model` types, App Group container as default location falling back to Application Support via `StorageDirectoryProviding`. `fetchMostRecentNoteByPerson()` is locked as one grouped `FetchDescriptor<NoteRecord>` sorted by `createdAt`, grouped in-memory by `personID` — explicitly not N per-person queries, called out as an implementation constraint so it can't quietly degrade later. `deleteAllData()` is one atomic repository method (not a loop over per-person deletes), so Settings' full-reset can't orphan a `RitualSessionRecord`.

**Repository shape (the round's one real decision):** operation-specific, not generic CRUD — `addPerson(name:cadence:firstNote:...)`, `logOutreach(personID:text:...)`, `snoozePerson`, `archivePerson`, `deletePersonPermanently`, `deleteAllData`, plus the ritual-session lifecycle methods (`fetchUnresolvedRitualSession`, `createRitualSession`, `resolveRitualItem`, `completeRitualSession`). Invariants live inside these methods, not scattered across view models.

**Testing strategy:** carried forward from `ios_architecture_review` and now concretized — pure unit tests on `WarmthCalculator`/`AttentionQueueBuilder` with zero SwiftData/main-actor involvement, SwiftData integration tests against in-memory containers (round-trip, App Group fallback, grouped-fetch correctness), StoreKit/UI journey tests via headless `StoreKitTest`/`SKTestSession`. `AuthenticationService.authenticate` returns `Result<Void, NurtureError>` specifically so cancelled-vs-failed auth is testable as distinct states, not a bare bool.

{"interfaces": [
  {"name": "PersonID", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct PersonID: Hashable, Codable, Sendable { let rawValue: UUID }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Canonical identity everywhere above SwiftData; lives in NurtureDomain (Foundation-only target)."},
  {"name": "CadenceTier", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum CadenceTier: String, Codable, Sendable, CaseIterable { case close, regular, occasional; var days: Int { get } }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "close=14, regular=42, occasional=180. Locked in detailed_discussion."},
  {"name": "WarmthState", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum WarmthState: String, Codable, Sendable { case warm, cooling, cold }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "warm<1.0, cooling 1.0-<1.5, cold>=1.5. Never stored, always derived at read time."},
  {"name": "ResolutionStatus", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ResolutionStatus: Codable, Sendable, Equatable { case unresolved; case loggedOutreach(noteID: UUID); case snoozed(until: Date); case archived }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Per-ritual-item outcome, persisted so an interrupted session resumes with exact progress."},
  {"name": "PersonSnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct PersonSnapshot: Identifiable, Sendable, Equatable { let id: PersonID; var name: String; var createdAt: Date; var lastInteractionAt: Date; var cadence: CadenceTier; var isArchived: Bool; var snoozedUntil: Date? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Plain value returned across the repository boundary; UI/domain never touch PersistentModel."},
  {"name": "NoteSnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct NoteSnapshot: Identifiable, Sendable, Equatable { let id: UUID; let personID: PersonID; var text: String; var createdAt: Date; var keyFact: String?; var openLoop: String? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Timeline item; source for why-now explanation anchors."},
  {"name": "WarmthAssessment", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct WarmthAssessment: Sendable, Equatable { let ratio: Double; let state: WarmthState; let explanation: String }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Live-derived output shared by dashboard, ritual snapshot capture, and detail screen."},
  {"name": "QueueEntry", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct QueueEntry: Identifiable, Sendable, Equatable { var id: PersonID { personID }; let personID: PersonID; let assessment: WarmthAssessment }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Single source for ranked attention/ritual queue membership."},
  {"name": "VisibleQueue", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct VisibleQueue: Sendable, Equatable { let visible: [QueueEntry]; let hiddenCount: Int }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Shared entitlement-truncated shape so dashboard teaser and ritual reveal-in-place cannot drift."},
  {"name": "RitualSessionItemSnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct RitualSessionItemSnapshot: Identifiable, Sendable, Equatable { let id: UUID; let personID: PersonID; let orderIndex: Int; let ratioAtEntry: Double; let stateAtEntry: WarmthState; let explanationAtEntry: String; var resolution: ResolutionStatus }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "orderIndex is mandatory — SwiftData relationship arrays don't guarantee order across relaunch."},
  {"name": "RitualSessionSnapshot", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct RitualSessionSnapshot: Identifiable, Sendable, Equatable { let id: UUID; let createdAt: Date; var completedAt: Date?; var items: [RitualSessionItemSnapshot] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "At most one row with completedAt == nil at a time."},
  {"name": "WarmthCalculator.evaluate", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "static func evaluate(person: PersonSnapshot, mostRecentNote: NoteSnapshot?, asOf referenceDate: Date, calendar: Calendar) -> WarmthAssessment", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Pure, Foundation-only. If this file imports SwiftData, that's a defect."},
  {"name": "AttentionQueueBuilder.buildQueue", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "static func buildQueue(people: [PersonSnapshot], mostRecentNotes: [PersonID: NoteSnapshot], asOf referenceDate: Date, calendar: Calendar, cap: Int) -> [QueueEntry]", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Ranked queue for both dashboard and ritual entry from one code path."},
  {"name": "AttentionQueueBuilder.applyEntitlement", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "static func applyEntitlement(to queue: [QueueEntry], entitlement: Entitlement, freeLimit: Int) -> VisibleQueue", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Single authority for the 3-vs-4 teaser boundary and mid-ritual reveal-in-place; never duplicated in view code."},
  {"name": "RitualSessionFactory.makeSnapshot", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "static func makeSnapshot(from queue: [QueueEntry], createdAt: Date) -> RitualSessionSnapshot", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Freezes membership, order, and explanation text at ritual entry."},
  {"name": "SchemaV1", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum SchemaV1: VersionedSchema { static var versionIdentifier: Schema.Version; static var models: [any PersistentModel.Type] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Versioned from first commit; no CloudKit-backed ModelConfiguration anywhere."},
  {"name": "PersonRecord", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class PersonRecord { var id: UUID; var name: String; var createdAt: Date; var lastInteractionAt: Date; var cadenceRaw: String; var isArchived: Bool; var snoozedUntil: Date?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var notes: [NoteRecord] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Lives only in NurtureData target."},
  {"name": "NoteRecord", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class NoteRecord { var id: UUID; var personID: UUID; var text: String; var createdAt: Date; var keyFact: String?; var openLoop: String? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Fetched via one grouped FetchDescriptor sorted by createdAt for fetchMostRecentNoteByPerson, never N per-person queries."},
  {"name": "RitualSessionRecord", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class RitualSessionRecord { var id: UUID; var createdAt: Date; var completedAt: Date?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var items: [RitualSessionItemRecord] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Persisted frozen session for interrupt/resume."},
  {"name": "RitualSessionItemRecord", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class RitualSessionItemRecord { var id: UUID; var personID: UUID; var orderIndex: Int; var ratioAtEntry: Double; var stateRaw: String; var explanationAtEntry: String; var resolutionRaw: String; var resolvedAt: Date?; var snoozeUntilIfSnoozed: Date? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Must always be fetched sorted by orderIndex; never trust relationship-array order."},
  {"name": "RelationshipRepository", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "@MainActor protocol RelationshipRepository { func fetchActivePeople() async throws -> [PersonSnapshot]; func fetchArchivedPeople() async throws -> [PersonSnapshot]; func fetchPerson(id: PersonID) async throws -> PersonSnapshot?; func fetchNotes(for personID: PersonID) async throws -> [NoteSnapshot]; func fetchMostRecentNoteByPerson() async throws -> [PersonID: NoteSnapshot]; func addPerson(name: String, cadence: CadenceTier, firstNote: String, keyFact: String?, openLoop: String?, at date: Date) async throws -> PersonID; func logOutreach(personID: PersonID, text: String, keyFact: String?, openLoop: String?, at date: Date) async throws; func snoozePerson(_ personID: PersonID, until date: Date) async throws; func archivePerson(_ personID: PersonID) async throws; func unarchivePerson(_ personID: PersonID) async throws; func deletePersonPermanently(_ personID: PersonID) async throws; func deleteAllData() async throws; func nameCollisionExists(for proposedName: String, excluding personID: PersonID?) async throws -> Bool; func fetchUnresolvedRitualSession() async throws -> RitualSessionSnapshot?; func createRitualSession(_ session: RitualSessionSnapshot) async throws; func resolveRitualItem(sessionID: UUID, personID: PersonID, resolution: ResolutionStatus, at date: Date) async throws; func completeRitualSession(sessionID: UUID, at date: Date) async throws }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Operation-specific by design: invariants (required first note, lastInteractionAt bump on outreach, duplicate check) live in one place, not scattered across view models. Protocol lives in NurtureDomain; concrete SwiftData implementation lives in NurtureData."},
  {"name": "ExportDocumentV1", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct ExportDocumentV1: Codable, Sendable { let schemaVersion: Int; let exportedAt: Date; let people: [PersonSnapshot]; let notes: [NoteSnapshot] }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Versioned plaintext export payload; never export raw SwiftData models."},
  {"name": "ExportService", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol ExportService: Sendable { func buildExport() async throws -> ExportDocumentV1 }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Called only after fresh re-auth and on-screen unencrypted-file warning."},
  {"name": "DateProvider", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol DateProvider: Sendable { func now() -> Date }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Injectable clock powering domain logic, debug time travel, and relock timing."},
  {"name": "AuthenticationService", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol AuthenticationService: Sendable { var biometricsOrPasscodeEnrolled: Bool { get }; func authenticate(reason: String) async -> Result<Void, NurtureError> }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Wraps LocalAuthentication deviceOwnerAuthentication; distinguishes cancel vs failure via NurtureError."},
  {"name": "Entitlement", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum Entitlement: Sendable, Equatable { case free; case subscribed(expiresAt: Date?) }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Richer than a flat bool so UI/tests express real transitions."},
  {"name": "EntitlementService", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "@MainActor protocol EntitlementService: AnyObject { var current: Entitlement { get }; func refresh() async; func purchase(productID: String) async throws; func restorePurchases() async throws; func startObservingTransactions() }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "StoreKit 2-backed; transaction observation is an in-process Task at launch, no Background Modes."},
  {"name": "NotificationScheduler", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol NotificationScheduler: Sendable { func requestAuthorizationIfNeeded() async -> Bool; func scheduleWeeklyReminder(on weekday: Int, hour: Int, minute: Int) async throws; func cancelWeeklyReminder() async }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Should-have; denied state falls back to in-app badge/banner."},
  {"name": "StorageDirectoryProviding", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol StorageDirectoryProviding: Sendable { func databaseURL() throws -> URL }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Abstracts App Group vs Application Support fallback so both paths are unit-testable."},
  {"name": "AppDependencies", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct AppDependencies { let repository: RelationshipRepository; let dateProvider: DateProvider; let authService: AuthenticationService; let entitlementService: EntitlementService; let notificationScheduler: NotificationScheduler; let exportService: ExportService }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Injected once at app root via environment."},
  {"name": "Route", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum Route: Hashable { case personDetail(PersonID); case settings; case archivedPeople }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Carries PersonID, never PersistentIdentifier."},
  {"name": "PaywallSource", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum PaywallSource: Identifiable { case dashboardTeaser; case ritualOverflow; var id: String { get } }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Single source of truth for paywall presentation and copy variant; no separate presentPaywall boolean."},
  {"name": "AppRouter", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@MainActor @Observable final class AppRouter { var path: [Route]; var addPersonPresented: Bool; var ritualPresented: Bool; var paywallSource: PaywallSource? }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Owns all push/modal presentation state; nothing else may present navigation."},
  {"name": "AttentionCardActions", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum AttentionCardActions { case navigate(() -> Void); case ritual(onLog: () -> Void, onSnooze: () -> Void, onArchive: () -> Void) }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Parameterizes the one shared AttentionCard view without boolean-flag sprawl."},
  {"name": "PrivacyShieldState", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@MainActor @Observable final class PrivacyShieldState { var isShielded: Bool; func handle(scenePhase: ScenePhase, now: Date) }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Root-level testable app-switcher privacy mask, ~2min relock grace."},
  {"name": "DebugClock", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "#if DEBUG final class DebugClock: DateProvider { var override: Date?; func advance(by component: Calendar.Component, value: Int); func set(_ date: Date?); func now() -> Date } #endif", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Discrete jumps only, no scrubber; compiled out of Release entirely, verified absent from a Release archive."},
  {"name": "NurtureError", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum NurtureError: Error, Equatable { case persistenceFailure(String); case authenticationCancelled; case authenticationFailed; case entitlementUnavailable; case exportFailed(String); case invalidSnoozeDate }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Unified error surface mapped to inline empty/error screen states, not raw system alerts."}
]}

**Quality Gate (Claude) — after round 2**

QUALITY: PASS

## Feedback

This clears the bar. All six required outputs are present and concrete: architecture overview (two-package split — `NurtureDomain` pure/Foundation-only, `NurtureData` SwiftData/StoreKit/LocalAuthentication concretes — with an enforceable, checkable rule "if scoring logic imports SwiftData, that's a defect"), file/module layout, data models (DTOs/snapshots distinct from `@Model` records, `orderIndex` fix for SwiftData relationship-array ordering), persistence strategy (`SchemaV1` versioned from commit one, App Group→Application Support fallback, atomic `deleteAllData`), and a three-tier testing strategy tied to specific named boundary cases carried forward from earlier phases (ratio 1.0/1.5 cutovers, day-zero-at-creation, 3-vs-4 paywall teaser, snooze-doesn't-reset-clock).

The round's one substantive fork — generic CRUD repository vs. operation-specific repository — is resolved with reasoning (invariants like required-first-note, `lastInteractionAt` bump, duplicate-name check need one enforced home, not scattered view-model logic), and it's consistent with everything locked in `ios_architecture_review` (pure value-type scoring boundary, frozen-content/live-entitlement ritual split, no CloudKit, zero third-party deps, DEBUG-only discrete-jump clock).

A single well-formed `interfaces-json` block closes out the coordinator's Final Output, covering all four owning_lane values (data_domain, primary_ui, services_utilities, polish_resilience) with concrete Swift signatures — sufficient for parallel build lanes to code against without inventing incompatible types. Minor cosmetic slop (Gemini's round-2 message being mostly narrated tool-call scratchpad rather than substantive content, a few `kind: "struct"` labels standing in for `final class`/`enum` since the schema only allows struct|protocol|function|enum|endpoint) doesn't rise to a blocking defect — the coordinator already flagged the process issue rather than papering over it, and the type-kind mismatches don't create ambiguity for a builder.

No contradictions with prior locked phases; ready to advance.

## Coordinator Decision

See the coordinator's message above.

## Final Output

Looking at this round, the two people doing real technical work — Codex and Claude — converged tightly and in detail, closing out the one open question from earlier in the phase. Gemini's round-2 contribution is, unfortunately, mostly narrated tool-call scratchpad text (file searches, transcript-log digging, a claim to have unilaterally written `technical_architecture_notes.md` to disk and declared the phase "locked") rather than substantive engagement with the specific fork Claude asked it to weigh in on. That said, the "Summary of Completed Work" Gemini did produce — two-package split, `SchemaV1` with `orderIndex`, pure `WarmthCalculator`/`AttentionQueueBuilder`, `PrivacyShieldState`, live `EntitlementService`, three-tier test plan — matches what Codex and Claude locked rather than contradicting it. Consistent with how this group has resolved silence in every prior phase, I'm reading that as non-objection, not a live dispute. One thing I want on the record as a process note rather than a blocker: a participant unilaterally declaring files "written" and the phase "ready for the next stage" isn't this phase's call to make — that's the coordinator's job, and actual file creation belongs to the build phase, not this discussion.

On substance, the phase closed its one real fight cleanly: Claude pushed back hard on any CRUD-shaped repository (`savePerson`/`saveNote`) because it lets invariants (required first note, `lastInteractionAt` bumping on outreach, duplicate checks) leak into view models instead of living in one enforced place. Codex's operation-specific repository shape already matched that instinct, and Gemini didn't defend the generic-CRUD alternative when asked directly a second time — so the operation-specific repository stands as the agreed shape. Claude also added a real, previously-missing implementation constraint (grouped single-fetch for `fetchMostRecentNoteByPerson`, never N per-person queries) and a two-package split (`NurtureDomain` — Foundation-only, holds every DTO/enum/protocol/pure function; `NurtureData` — SwiftData/StoreKit/LocalAuthentication/UserNotifications concrete implementations), both of which Codex's structure already anticipated and nobody contested.

CONSENSUS: YES

## Final Output

**Architecture overview:** MVVM-ish app target on top of two local Swift packages. `NurtureDomain` is pure Foundation — every DTO, enum, and protocol, plus `WarmthCalculator` and `AttentionQueueBuilder` as non-actor-isolated pure functions. The enforceable rule: if a file with scoring/queue-building logic imports SwiftData, that's a defect. `NurtureData` holds the concrete SwiftData repository, StoreKit 2 entitlement service, LocalAuthentication wrapper, and notification scheduler. The app target owns `AppRouter` (single `NavigationStack` path plus `addPersonPresented`/`ritualPresented`/`paywallSource` — nothing else may present navigation), views, and the `PrivacyShieldState`/`DebugClock` polish layer. This split isn't just testing hygiene — it's stated explicitly as a free side benefit: the domain layer becomes droppable into a future watchOS/extension target with zero rework, at no cost beyond what QA already required.

**File/module layout:** `NurtureDomain` (data_domain types + protocols), `NurtureData` (SwiftData `@Model` records, concrete repository, StoreKit/auth/notification adapters), app target (`primary_ui` router/views, `polish_resilience` shield/debug tooling).

**Data models:** `PersonSnapshot`/`NoteSnapshot` as the only shapes crossing the repository boundary (never `PersistentModel`); `WarmthAssessment`/`QueueEntry`/`VisibleQueue` as the single shared shape between dashboard and ritual so entitlement truncation can't drift between the two call sites; `RitualSessionSnapshot`/`RitualSessionItemSnapshot` with a mandatory `orderIndex` (SwiftData relationship arrays don't guarantee order across relaunch) and a `ResolutionStatus` enum carrying per-item outcome for resume-after-termination.

**Persistence strategy:** `SchemaV1: VersionedSchema` from the first commit, no CloudKit-backed configuration anywhere. `PersonRecord`/`NoteRecord`/`RitualSessionRecord`/`RitualSessionItemRecord` as the `@Model` types, App Group container as default location falling back to Application Support via `StorageDirectoryProviding`. `fetchMostRecentNoteByPerson()` is locked as one grouped `FetchDescriptor<NoteRecord>` sorted by `createdAt`, grouped in-memory by `personID` — explicitly not N per-person queries, called out as an implementation constraint so it can't quietly degrade later. `deleteAllData()` is one atomic repository method (not a loop over per-person deletes), so Settings' full-reset can't orphan a `RitualSessionRecord`.

**Repository shape (the round's one real decision):** operation-specific, not generic CRUD — `addPerson(name:cadence:firstNote:...)`, `logOutreach(personID:text:...)`, `snoozePerson`, `archivePerson`, `deletePersonPermanently`, `deleteAllData`, plus the ritual-session lifecycle methods (`fetchUnresolvedRitualSession`, `createRitualSession`, `resolveRitualItem`, `completeRitualSession`). Invariants live inside these methods, not scattered across view models.

**Testing strategy:** carried forward from `ios_architecture_review` and now concretized — pure unit tests on `WarmthCalculator`/`AttentionQueueBuilder` with zero SwiftData/main-actor involvement, SwiftData integration tests against in-memory containers (round-trip, App Group fallback, grouped-fetch correctness), StoreKit/UI journey tests via headless `StoreKitTest`/`SKTestSession`. `AuthenticationService.authenticate` returns `Result<Void, NurtureError>` specifically so cancelled-vs-failed auth is testable as distinct states, not a bare bool.

  {"name": "RitualSessionFactory.makeSnapshot", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "static func makeSnapshot(from queue: [QueueEntry], createdAt: Date) -> RitualSessionSnapshot", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Freezes membership, order, and explanation text at ritual entry."},
  {"name": "SchemaV1", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum SchemaV1: VersionedSchema { static var versionIdentifier: Schema.Version; static var models: [any PersistentModel.Type] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Versioned from first commit; no CloudKit-backed ModelConfiguration anywhere."},
  {"name": "PersonRecord", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class PersonRecord { var id: UUID; var name: String; var createdAt: Date; var lastInteractionAt: Date; var cadenceRaw: String; var isArchived: Bool; var snoozedUntil: Date?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var notes: [NoteRecord] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Lives only in NurtureData target."},
  {"name": "NoteRecord", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class NoteRecord { var id: UUID; var personID: UUID; var text: String; var createdAt: Date; var keyFact: String?; var openLoop: String? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Fetched via one grouped FetchDescriptor sorted by createdAt for fetchMostRecentNoteByPerson, never N per-person queries."},
  {"name": "RitualSessionRecord", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class RitualSessionRecord { var id: UUID; var createdAt: Date; var completedAt: Date?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var items: [RitualSessionItemRecord] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Persisted frozen session for interrupt/resume."},
  {"name": "RitualSessionItemRecord", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class RitualSessionItemRecord { var id: UUID; var personID: UUID; var orderIndex: Int; var ratioAtEntry: Double; var stateRaw: String; var explanationAtEntry: String; var resolutionRaw: String; var resolvedAt: Date?; var snoozeUntilIfSnoozed: Date? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Must always be fetched sorted by orderIndex; never trust relationship-array order."},
  {"name": "RelationshipRepository", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "@MainActor protocol RelationshipRepository { func fetchActivePeople() async throws -> [PersonSnapshot]; func fetchArchivedPeople() async throws -> [PersonSnapshot]; func fetchPerson(id: PersonID) async throws -> PersonSnapshot?; func fetchNotes(for personID: PersonID) async throws -> [NoteSnapshot]; func fetchMostRecentNoteByPerson() async throws -> [PersonID: NoteSnapshot]; func addPerson(name: String, cadence: CadenceTier, firstNote: String, keyFact: String?, openLoop: String?, at date: Date) async throws -> PersonID; func logOutreach(personID: PersonID, text: String, keyFact: String?, openLoop: String?, at date: Date) async throws; func snoozePerson(_ personID: PersonID, until date: Date) async throws; func archivePerson(_ personID: PersonID) async throws; func unarchivePerson(_ personID: PersonID) async throws; func deletePersonPermanently(_ personID: PersonID) async throws; func deleteAllData() async throws; func nameCollisionExists(for proposedName: String, excluding personID: PersonID?) async throws -> Bool; func fetchUnresolvedRitualSession() async throws -> RitualSessionSnapshot?; func createRitualSession(_ session: RitualSessionSnapshot) async throws; func resolveRitualItem(sessionID: UUID, personID: PersonID, resolution: ResolutionStatus, at date: Date) async throws; func completeRitualSession(sessionID: UUID, at date: Date) async throws }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Operation-specific by design: invariants (required first note, lastInteractionAt bump on outreach, duplicate check) live in one place, not scattered across view models. Protocol lives in NurtureDomain; concrete SwiftData implementation lives in NurtureData."},
  {"name": "ExportDocumentV1", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct ExportDocumentV1: Codable, Sendable { let schemaVersion: Int; let exportedAt: Date; let people: [PersonSnapshot]; let notes: [NoteSnapshot] }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Versioned plaintext export payload; never export raw SwiftData models."},
  {"name": "ExportService", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol ExportService: Sendable { func buildExport() async throws -> ExportDocumentV1 }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Called only after fresh re-auth and on-screen unencrypted-file warning."},
  {"name": "DateProvider", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol DateProvider: Sendable { func now() -> Date }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Injectable clock powering domain logic, debug time travel, and relock timing."},
  {"name": "AuthenticationService", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol AuthenticationService: Sendable { var biometricsOrPasscodeEnrolled: Bool { get }; func authenticate(reason: String) async -> Result<Void, NurtureError> }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Wraps LocalAuthentication deviceOwnerAuthentication; distinguishes cancel vs failure via NurtureError."},
  {"name": "Entitlement", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum Entitlement: Sendable, Equatable { case free; case subscribed(expiresAt: Date?) }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Richer than a flat bool so UI/tests express real transitions."},
  {"name": "EntitlementService", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "@MainActor protocol EntitlementService: AnyObject { var current: Entitlement { get }; func refresh() async; func purchase(productID: String) async throws; func restorePurchases() async throws; func startObservingTransactions() }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "StoreKit 2-backed; transaction observation is an in-process Task at launch, no Background Modes."},
  {"name": "NotificationScheduler", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol NotificationScheduler: Sendable { func requestAuthorizationIfNeeded() async -> Bool; func scheduleWeeklyReminder(on weekday: Int, hour: Int, minute: Int) async throws; func cancelWeeklyReminder() async }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Should-have; denied state falls back to in-app badge/banner."},
  {"name": "StorageDirectoryProviding", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol StorageDirectoryProviding: Sendable { func databaseURL() throws -> URL }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Abstracts App Group vs Application Support fallback so both paths are unit-testable."},
  {"name": "AppDependencies", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct AppDependencies { let repository: RelationshipRepository; let dateProvider: DateProvider; let authService: AuthenticationService; let entitlementService: EntitlementService; let notificationScheduler: NotificationScheduler; let exportService: ExportService }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Injected once at app root via environment."},
  {"name": "Route", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum Route: Hashable { case personDetail(PersonID); case settings; case archivedPeople }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Carries PersonID, never PersistentIdentifier."},
  {"name": "PaywallSource", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum PaywallSource: Identifiable { case dashboardTeaser; case ritualOverflow; var id: String { get } }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Single source of truth for paywall presentation and copy variant; no separate presentPaywall boolean."},
  {"name": "AppRouter", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@MainActor @Observable final class AppRouter { var path: [Route]; var addPersonPresented: Bool; var ritualPresented: Bool; var paywallSource: PaywallSource? }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Owns all push/modal presentation state; nothing else may present navigation."},
  {"name": "AttentionCardActions", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum AttentionCardActions { case navigate(() -> Void); case ritual(onLog: () -> Void, onSnooze: () -> Void, onArchive: () -> Void) }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Parameterizes the one shared AttentionCard view without boolean-flag sprawl."},
  {"name": "PrivacyShieldState", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@MainActor @Observable final class PrivacyShieldState { var isShielded: Bool; func handle(scenePhase: ScenePhase, now: Date) }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Root-level testable app-switcher privacy mask, ~2min relock grace."},
  {"name": "DebugClock", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "#if DEBUG final class DebugClock: DateProvider { var override: Date?; func advance(by component: Calendar.Component, value: Int); func set(_ date: Date?); func now() -> Date } #endif", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Discrete jumps only, no scrubber; compiled out of Release entirely, verified absent from a Release archive."},
  {"name": "NurtureError", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum NurtureError: Error, Equatable { case persistenceFailure(String); case authenticationCancelled; case authenticationFailed; case entitlementUnavailable; case exportFailed(String); case invalidSnoozeDate }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Unified error surface mapped to inline empty/error screen states, not raw system alerts."}
]}
